title: -Donation for the Thousand Oaks School Park Improvement Project in Memory of Michael Seltzer

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution accepting a cash donation of up to $80,000 for the Thousand Oaks
School Park Improvement Project in memory of Michael Seltzer.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
This cash donation in the amount of up to $80,000 from the friends of Michael Seltzer
will be deposited into the Parks Fund donation revenue budget code 138-52-545-000-
0000-000-000-481110.
sections.current_situation:
The friends of Michael Seltzer wish to provide a donation of up to $80,000 to fund the
Thousand Oaks School Park Improvement Project. The City’s Open Governance
Ordinance (OGO) requires City Council approval of any gift to the City in excess of
$1,000 (BMC Section 2.06.150, Ord. 7,166- N.S.) Without this donation, the project
would not be possible.
sections.background:
Col. Michael “Mike” Seltzer, a Berkeley-reared Air Force fighter pilot and father of three
children, died of lung cancer in 2023. Seltzer grew up attending Berkeley schools and
graduated from UC Berkeley in 2002, where he enrolled in the Air Force ROTC program
and earned a bachelor’s degree in physical sciences. He later deployed to Iraq and
Afghanistan, rose to become a colonel, and trained dozens of F-16 and F-35
pilots. Additional details about Michael Seltzer and the proposed project can be found in
the Berkeleyside article at the following link:
https://www.berkeleyside.org/2025/03/31/airplane-playground-air-force-pilot-michael-
seltzer-burn-pits.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.

Donation for the Thousand Oaks School Park Improvement Project CONSENT CALENDAR
In Memory of Michael Seltzer December 2, 2025
sections.rationale:
This donation will cover the cost of the design and implementation of the Thousand
Oaks School Park Improvement Project, without which this project would not be
possible.
sections.alternatives:
None
CONTACT PERSON
Scott Ferris, Director, Parks, Recreation, and Waterfront, 510-981-6711
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 2
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ACCEPTING A CASH DONATION OF UP TO $80,000 FOR THE THOUSAND OAKS
SCHOOL PARK IMPROVEMENT PROJECT IN MEMORY OF MICHAEL SELTZER
WHEREAS, Col. Michael “Mike” Seltzer, a Berkeley-reared Air Force fighter pilot and
father of three children, died of lung cancer in 2023; and
WHEREAS, Seltzer grew up attending Berkeley schools and graduated from UC Berkeley
in 2002, where he enrolled in the Air Force ROTC program and earned a bachelor’s
degree in physical sciences. He later deployed to Iraq and Afghanistan, rose to become
a colonel, and trained dozens of F-16 and F-35 pilots. Additional details can be found in
the Berkeleyside article at the following link:
https://www.berkeleyside.org/2025/03/31/airplane-playground-air-force-pilot-michael-
seltzer-burn-pits; and
WHEREAS, the friends of Michael Seltzer wish to provide a donation of up to $80,000 to
fund the Thousand Oaks School Park Improvement Project; and
WHEREAS, in 2026, the City will implement the Thousand Oaks School Park
Improvement Project that will include a community process; and
WHEREAS, without the donation from the friends of Michael Seltzer, this third phase of
the project would not be possible; and
WHEREAS, the cash donation in the amount of up to $80,000 from the friends of Michael
Seltzer will be deposited into the Parks Fund donation revenue budget code 138-52-545-
000-0000-000-000-481110; and
WHEREAS, the City’s Open Governance Ordinance (OGO) requires City Council
approval of any gift to the City in excess of $1,000 (BMC Section 2.06.150, Ord. 7,166-
N.S.).
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley to accept
a cash donation of up to $80,000 from the friends of Michael Seltzer for the Thousand
Oaks School Park Improvement Project in his memory.
